notes: Paper is a QSPR/ANN machine-learning methodology paper. It trains models on ~1,705 DIPPR compounds for TC, PC, ACEN, NBP. All per-compound experimental values reside in DIPPR or in Supplementary Material 2 (Excel file) and are NOT provided in the main text/NXML. The main text contains only: (a) aggregate dataset statistics (Table 1: mean/SD/quartiles for TC, PC, ACEN, NBP over 1705 molecules); (b) aggregate model performance metrics (Table 3: MAE/MAPE/R^2); (c) Table 4 and Table 5 which DO contain per-compound TC/PC/NBP values, but the compound identity in every row is rendered as an <inline-graphic> chemical-structure image (filenames Figa-Figm in Table 4, Fign in Table 5) -- no compound names appear in the table cells, caption, or surrounding prose. The text only refers to compounds qualitatively (e.g., 'fluorinated and sulphurated compounds', 'dinitriles NC(CH2)nCN'). N-alkane property trends (Figs 8, 9) are shown vs carbon count without per-compound names. (d) Table 6 is aggregate ICAS-vs-LRGP statistics. (e) Tables 2 and 7 are not property values. Hence there is no per-compound NAMED mp/bp/critical value in any main-text or NXML location. Per-compound data exists only in SI (Excel file not provided to extractor) and as figure-only structures. Therefore 0 rows was the CORRECT extraction decision.
